_comment: Parallel build lanes as VS Code INTEGRATED-TERMINAL tabs. PREFERRED way to launch is the shell command `overnight-build N` (alias `ob N`) from any terminal - it jumps to cadence-lane-N and starts the lane. These tasks are the click-to-launch alternative: Cmd+Shift+P -> Tasks: Run Task -> pick a lane, or `Lanes 0-4: open all` to spawn all five at once. Each opens a dedicated integrated terminal in cadence-lane-N. Model (Opus 4.8 1M) + effort (xhigh / ultracode) come from each worktree's .claude/settings.local.json. The args string is deliberately free of quotes and parentheses so the shell never mis-parses it.

tasks.label: Lane 0
args: Operate at ultracode: xhigh effort and use the Workflow tool for substantive multi-step work. You are parallel build Lane 0 in this worktree. Read .remember/LANE.md and the autonomous-build-loop playbook sections 15 and 16, then run the continuous lane loop in this terminal: each cycle, git fetch and rebase on origin/main, run scripts/lane.sh reap, claim the next highest-impact eligible item from feature-dashboard.md atomically with scripts/lane.sh before writing any code, build, gate with tsc and build and tests, adversarially review, run the doc-loop, commit with a clear WHY, fast-forward push, release the claim, then immediately continue. Never stop after one item. If the board is dry, note it and recheck in about 25 minutes. Stay in your lane via the ledger.
